ground_truth_fix: Also your code has the same problem as OP's. You are not actually consuming the logs. For posterity your init call is here: https://github.com/firezone/firezone/blob/683a190855509df8776f3ff8d37cc638dc272e8c/rust/relay/server/src/ebpf/linux.rs#L47 but you drop the returned Logger which is almost certainly what's leading to your (and OP's) problems. See https://github.com/aya-rs/aya/tree/main/aya-log#user-space-code. With that, closing this. Please let me know if this doesn't solve the problem.

prompt_b:
Here is eBPF code that fails verification. Fix it.

Code:
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    TermLogger::init(
        LevelFilter::Debug,
        ConfigBuilder::new()
            .set_time_level(LevelFilter::Off)
            .set_thread_level(LevelFilter::Off)
            .set_target_level(LevelFilter::Off)
            .set_location_level(LevelFilter::Off)
            .build(),
        TerminalMode::Stderr,
        ColorChoice::Auto,
    )?;

    let mut bpf = Ebpf::load(aya::include_bytes_aligned!(concat!(
        env!("OUT_DIR"),
        "/ebpf-execve"
    )))?;

    EbpfLogger::init(&mut bpf)?;

    let prog: &mut TracePoint = bpf
        .program_mut("sys_enter_execve")
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::Error::msg("failed to get program"))?
        .try_into()
        .context("converting the program to a TracePoint")?;
    prog.load().context("loading")?;
    prog.attach("syscalls", "sys_enter_execve")
        .context("attaching")?;

    eprintln!("Waiting for Ctrl-C...");
    signal::ctrl_c().await?;
    eprintln!("Exiting...");

    Ok(())
}
